title: A Hero's Birthday: Sir Nicholas Winton
story: A true hero was born in London on May 19, 1909. Sir Nicholas Winton would grow up to save hundreds of children from danger during World War II. He organized rescue missions that brought 669 kids to safety in England, away from the Nazis. People called him the 'British Schindler' for his brave actions. Winton's kindness and courage helped many families escape terrible times.
activity_question: What nickname was Sir Nicholas Winton given for his heroic actions?
follow_up_question: Why do you think it's important to remember people like Sir Nicholas Winton?

title: Comet Chaos: Earth's Close Encounter
story: Panic gripped the world as Earth passed through Halley's Comet's tail in 1910. On May 19, people feared poisonous gases would fill the air. Some even bought 'comet pills' and gas masks! title: Cosmic Dance: Planets Line Up

title: Mars 2: A Crash Course in Space Exploration
story: Whoosh! In 1971, the Soviet Union sent a daring spacecraft named Mars 2 hurtling towards the Red Planet. On May 19, it became the first human-made object to reach Mars - but not quite as planned. Instead of a gentle touchdown, Mars 2 crash-landed on the dusty surface. While it wasn't the smooth arrival scientists hoped for, this bold mission paved the way for future Mars explorers. It showed us just how tricky landing on other planets can be!
activity_question: What happened to the Mars 2 spacecraft when it reached Mars?
activity_choices: It turned into a Mars rover
follow_up_question: Why do you think landing on Mars is so challenging for spacecraft?

title: Curly Neal: Harlem Globetrotters' Dribbling Wizard
story: Curly Neal, the basketball magician, bounced into the world on May 19, 1942. Born in Greensboro, North Carolina, he grew up to become a legendary guard for the Harlem Globetrotters. title: Oilers Skate to Stanley Cup Glory
story: Hockey fans erupted in cheers as the Edmonton Oilers clinched their first Stanley Cup in 1984. On May 19, superstar Wayne Gretzky led the charge, scoring twice in a thrilling 5-2 victory over the New York Islanders. bonus_fact: The Stanley Cup is the oldest professional sports trophy in North America.

title: Planet Earth Takes a Breath
story: The world hit pause in 2020, and our planet noticed! Scientists discovered something amazing on May 19 that year. As people stayed home during lockdowns, greenhouse gas emissions plummeted. title: Tiny Toxins, Big Problem
story: Scientists made a surprising discovery in 2022. They found that tiny particles called 'obesogens' might be making people gain weight. These sneaky toxins come from pollution in our environment. The really wild part? These obesogens can be passed down from parents to kids, like a tricky family heirloom nobody wants. This news on May 19 changed how we think about the obesity puzzle.
activity_question: What did scientists discover about obesogens?
activity_choices: They can be passed down from parents to kids
They make people lose weight quickly
They come from eating too much sugar
activity_answer: They can be passed down from parents to kids

title: Ringing in Safety: The Electric Fire Alarm
story: Ding-dong! A new sound of safety echoed through history. On May 19, 1857, Americans William Francis Channing and Moses G. Farmer patented the electric fire alarm. It was a spark of genius that would save countless lives in the years to come.

title: A French Space Pioneer Takes Flight
story: Jean-Pierre Haigneré soared into history on May 19, 1948. Born in Paris, this future French cosmonaut would become the first non-Russian flight engineer on a Soyuz mission. Haigneré's journey from Earth to the stars showed that space exploration knows no borders. His groundbreaking role on Soyuz TM-29 opened doors for international cooperation high above our planet.

title: Sax Star Tom Scott Hits the Scene
story: Smooth sax sounds filled the air when Tom Scott was born in Los Angeles in 1948. This future jazz-fusion star would go on to play with famous bands like L.A. Express and The Blues Brothers. Scott even jammed with Paul McCartney on the hit song 'Listen to What the Man Said'. His musical journey began on May 19, shaping the sound of jazz for years to come.
activity_question: Which famous musician did Tom Scott collaborate with on a hit song?
follow_up_question: What instrument is Tom Scott known for playing?

title: Victoria Wood: Britain's Beloved Comedy Queen
story: Laughter echoed through Bury, Lancashire as Victoria Wood entered the world in 1953. Born on May 19, this future comedy star would bring joy to millions. Wood became famous for her witty jokes and hilarious TV shows like 'Dinner Ladies'. Her talent for making people smile made her one of Britain's most cherished entertainers.

title: Astronaut Pierre Thuot Blasts into Life
story: Imagine soaring through space! Pierre J. Thuot, born in Groton, Connecticut on May 19, 1955, would grow up to do just that. This future astronaut joined the U.S. Navy and flew on three space shuttle missions. Thuot's adventures took him far beyond his hometown, all the way to the stars!
activity_question: What did Pierre J. Thuot grow up to become?
follow_up_question: Can you name one of the space shuttle missions Thuot flew on?
